general_information.institution: University of Guelph, Ontario Agricultural College
general_information.title_of_the_research_project: Evaluating Precision Feeding Strategies to Reduce Methane Emissions in High-Producing Dairy Cows Without Compromising Milk Yield

biography.brief_biography_line_1: Emily Patterson is an M.Sc. candidate in Animal Biosciences at the University of Guelph, focusing on sustainable dairy nutrition and greenhouse gas mitigation.
biography.brief_biography_line_2: Raised on her family’s 80-cow Holstein farm in eastern Ontario, she combines hands-on herd management experience with data-driven research in precision feeding.
biography.brief_biography_line_3: Her current work examines how fine-tuning protein and fiber ratios in total mixed rations can lower enteric methane while maintaining or improving milk production and cow health.
biography.brief_biography_line_4: Emily has worked as a summer research assistant in two dairy nutrition labs, co-authored an abstract for the ADSA annual meeting, and volunteers with 4-H dairy clubs mentoring youth.
biography.brief_biography_line_5: After completing her degree, she hopes to work as a dairy nutrition consultant and extension specialist, helping producers implement practical, science-based strategies for more sustainable milk production.
